prompt:
IAM platform ticket. Contractor onboarding is moving off manual Admin Console clicks: HR drops approved hires into our queue, and we need a small Java client library that stages each hire in Okta ahead of their start date, activates them on day one, and tells the ops channel exactly what went wrong when Okta says no. Plain JDK only (java.net.http is fine), no Maven, no SDK jars — this slots into our existing toolbox repo that builds with the source launcher. TestMain.java in this directory is the acceptance harness and runs a loopback fake Okta; it and everything under docs/ are protected — leave them untouched.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Okta docs — the management API reference for the Users API (the create operation and its activate query parameter, the User object with its status set and _links) and the user lifecycle operations (the activate call, its sendEmail parameter, what it returns, and which statuses it's legal from), plus Okta's error-codes reference so you know which errorCode means what. Only first-party Okta pages (developer.okta.com or Okta's own published API spec) count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; if the live docs contradict anything pinned there, say so in your summary and leave the protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the harness expects (design the rest yourself, ~4-7 source files, default package like the harness):
- UserLifecycleClient(String orgBaseUrl, String apiToken) with createStaged(Map<String,String> profileFields) -> OktaUser, activate(String userId, boolean sendEmail) -> ActivationResult, getUser(String userId) -> OktaUser, and waitForStatus(String userId, String targetStatus, int maxPolls, java.util.function.IntConsumer pacer) -> OktaUser. Staging a hire must NOT trip Okta's default create behavior — check what the docs say the create defaults to. waitForStatus re-GETs the user, calls pacer.accept(attempt) between consecutive polls (never sleeps), returns the user once the status matches, and throws OktaTransitionTimeout (message naming the user id and target status) once maxPolls reads are spent.
- OktaUser exposes id(), status(), transitioningToStatus() (null when absent), created(), activated() (null until set), statusChanged(), profile() as Map<String,String>, and links() as Map<String,String> of every HAL relation to its href, verbatim — Okta changes which relations exist as the lifecycle advances, and downstream tooling keys off them, so don't filter or rebuild hrefs.
- ActivationResult exposes activationToken() and activationUrl().
- Error taxonomy: OktaApiException (unchecked) exposing status(), errorCode(), errorSummary(), errorId(), errorCauses() as List<String> of each cause's errorSummary in order; subclasses OktaValidationException, OktaPermissionException, OktaTransitionException. Heads-up from the last on-call rotation: two of those come back with the SAME HTTP status, so pick your classification key accordingly — the docs' error-code reference is the map. Anything else non-2xx surfaces as the base type. Exception messages must never contain the API token.
- Wire details matter: the SSWS auth scheme, JSON accept/content types, profile fields nested where Okta wants them, and the lifecycle call as the documented sub-resource path.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — must end with the OK line. No real Okta org, no credentials, no sleeps anywhere.

research.product: Okta Workforce Identity Cloud — Users API
research.interface: Okta Core management REST API /api/v1 (official OpenAPI spec 2026.07.1)

official_sources.title: okta/okta-management-openapi-spec — Okta Admin Management API (version 2026.07.1)
official_sources.used_for: createUser (POST /api/v1/users, activate query param default true, CreateUserRequest with profile/credentials, 200 returns User); activateUser (POST /api/v1/users/{id}/lifecycle/activate, sendEmail default true, 200 UserActivationToken with activationToken/activationUrl, STAGED/DEPROVISIONED precondition, async transitioningToStatus=ACTIVE); getUser; UserStatus enum; User._links HAL semantics; Error schema and canned examples (E0000001 ApiValidationFailed with errorCauses, E0000006 AccessDenied, E0000007 ResourceNotFound)

official_sources.title: Okta API Error Codes | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: exact HTTP status and errorSummary per code: E0000001=400 validation, E0000006=403 permission, E0000016=403 'Activation failed because the user is already active' (transition), E0000007=404 not found; confirms both 403 codes must be told apart by errorCode

official_sources.title: Core Okta API | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: org base URL pattern https://{yourOktaDomain}/api/v1/..., SSWS Authorization scheme, application/json media types, error envelope fields errorCode/errorSummary/errorLink/errorId/errorCauses, X-Okta-Request-Id

official_sources.title: Users | Okta Developer (management API reference)
official_sources.used_for: canonical current reference location for the Users API operations; confirms activate/provider/nextLogin query params and the User response shape rendered from the same OpenAPI contract

research.language_rationale: User provisioning glue at this shop runs on the JVM alongside the HR importers; the Users API is documented as plain versioned REST (/api/v1) with JSON bodies and SSWS header auth, so java.net.http.HttpClient against the documented wire contract is a credible, dependency-free integration surface (Okta's Java management SDK is a wrapper generated from this same OpenAPI spec).
